skill.name: minimax-music-playlist
skill.description: Generate personalized music playlists by analyzing listening history, taste profiles, and user feedback, then create songs and album art with MiniMax tools.
skill.summary: Create personalized AI music playlists from listening history and feedback.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command and blocker patterns, but the blocker matches are false positives from Markdown prose and examples, not cryptographic code. The confirmed risks are legitimate but sensitive: the skill directs agents to inspect local listening data, search the home directory for Spotify exports, call external music APIs, cache profiles, and run media generation and playback commands.

medium_findings.title: Local Listening History Collection and Profiling
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to collect Apple Music data, use Spotify export files, derive taste profiles, and save profile data. This is legitimate for playlist personalization, but it processes sensitive listening history and should require clear user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly collect, analyze, and cache music listening data. The file also includes a privacy rule, so the risk is sensitive data handling rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Shell Commands With User-Influenced Values
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell command examples for music generation, image generation, local searches, app opening, playback, and playlist listing. These commands are expected for this workflow, but prompts, names, and paths may be influenced by user input and require safe argument handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the skill. They are not bundled executable scripts, but unsafe interpolation by an agent could create command injection or unintended file access.

medium_findings.title: External Artist Lookup May Disclose Music Preferences
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to query MusicBrainz for artists not found in the local mapping table. This is a normal enrichment step, but it can send artist names derived from local listening history to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external API endpoint and artist-name query flow are directly documented. The endpoint is a legitimate public music metadata service, so the concern is privacy disclosure rather than malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches occur in Markdown description, playlist examples, media file names, and command flags. No cryptographic implementation, hashing API, or encryption downgrade was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found these lines contain music-related prose, file extensions, or CLI examples. There is no code that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Spotify Export Search Is Broad but Bounded
low_findings.description: The skill searches within the user home directory for Spotify export files. The command is limited by max depth and specific file names, but it still inspects local filesystem metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact search command is present and scoped to Spotify export names. The workflow asks before using found data, reducing but not eliminating privacy risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Templates for Media Generation and Playback
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command templates using mmx, find, open, mpv, ffplay, afplay, and ls. These commands should be executed with structured arguments and user-visible consent for local data access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown in the instructions. The risk depends on runtime handling, because the skill itself is Markdown and does not execute them automatically.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Profile and Playlist File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill writes generated audio, cover art, playlist metadata, and cached taste profiles under local music and skill data directories. This is expected functionality, but it creates persistent user preference records.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write locations and metadata fields are documented directly. The writes are part of the product behavior, but they should be disclosed to users.

content.value_statement: Music generation can feel generic when it ignores a listener's history and mood. This skill builds a taste profile, plans a playlist, generates tracks, creates cover art, and saves feedback.
actual_capabilities: Builds a music taste profile from Apple Music data, Spotify exports, or manual input.
Summarizes genre, mood, vocal, tempo, language, and top artist preferences.
Plans themed playlists with genre mix, language choices, and per-song descriptions.
Uses MiniMax CLI commands to generate songs and square album cover art.
Plays generated MP3 files with local audio players when available.
Saves playlist metadata and feedback for future personalization.
limitations: Requires the mmx CLI, authentication, Python 3, and a local audio player for full automation.
Spotify history requires a user-provided data export; it is not read from Spotify locally.
MusicBrainz enrichment depends on network access and may miss unknown artists.
Generated quality depends on MiniMax model behavior and prompt specificity.

use_cases.title: Create Mood-Based Playlists
use_cases.description: Generate a short playlist for a theme such as commute, late night, rain, study, or workout.

use_cases.title: Prototype AI Music Directions
use_cases.description: Turn a listener profile into song concepts, generated tracks, and cover art for creative review.

use_cases.title: Explore Multilingual Taste Patterns
use_cases.description: Analyze language and genre preferences across Apple Music, Spotify exports, and manual notes.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Playlist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear theme.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-song playlist for a calm Sunday morning. Use my available music profile if it exists.

prompt_templates.title: Use Manual Taste Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: No local listening data is available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a personalized playlist from these tastes: Japanese city pop, mellow R&B, female vocals, and relaxed evening moods.

prompt_templates.title: Blend Sources and Feedback
prompt_templates.scenario: Use listening history and improve from prior ratings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan my available listening data, reuse recent feedback, and generate a commute playlist with more upbeat tracks than last time.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multilingual Concept Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a seven-track playlist that blends C-pop, J-pop, and instrumental lo-fi. Keep user-facing text in Chinese and generate the mmx prompts in English.

output_examples.input: Create a late night chill playlist.
output: A taste summary with main genres, moods, vocal balance, and top artists.
A five-track playlist plan with each song's genre, mood, vocal style, and short description.
Generated song files, cover art, playback details, and saved playlist metadata.

output_examples.input: Use my Spotify export and make something energetic.
output: A request to confirm use of the export if it is found locally.
A profile weighted by longer listening sessions and filtered to remove short skips.
An upbeat playlist plan with generated tracks and an album cover.

output_examples.input: Play one of my previous MiniMax playlists.
output: A list of saved playlist folders from the generated music directory.
Playback of the selected playlist in filename order, or file paths if no player is available.

best_practices: Confirm consent before scanning local music apps or using Spotify export files.
Use clear themes and song counts so the playlist plan matches the intended scene.
Review saved feedback after listening so future playlists improve.
anti_patterns: Do not expose raw listening history when an aggregated summary is enough.
Do not paste sensitive Spotify export fields into prompts or public messages.
Do not run generated shell commands with unescaped user-provided names or paths.

faq.question: Does this skill require MiniMax access?
faq.answer: Yes. Full generation requires the mmx CLI and an authenticated MiniMax account.

faq.question: Can it use Spotify listening history?
faq.answer: Yes, but only from a user-provided Spotify data export file. It does not read Spotify history locally.

faq.question: Does it send my listening history to external services?
faq.answer: It may query MusicBrainz with artist names for genre enrichment. The audit recommends clear consent before that step.

faq.question: Can it work without Apple Music or Spotify data?
faq.answer: Yes. You can describe your taste manually, and the skill can build a playlist from that description.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The skill saves generated songs and cover art under the local MiniMax playlist directory described in its instructions.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with warnings. Users should understand the local data access, command execution, network lookup, and profile caching behavior.
